scores.correctness_notes: Basic structure (base + platforms key, build-on/build-for) is correct, but the multi-base syntax is wrong. The gold standard shows embedding base in platform entries (ubuntu@22.04:amd64:) with no top-level base key, while the agent incorrectly suggests YAML multi-document syntax (---). Missing supported bases list (almalinux@9, ubuntu@24.10, ubuntu@25.04) and architectures.
scores.hallucination_notes: The YAML multi-document syntax (---) for multi-base builds is fabricated — charmcraft does not use YAML multi-doc syntax for this. The correct syntax embeds the base in platform entry names like ubuntu@22.04:amd64. The legacy bases format shown may be valid but uses different field names than gold standard.
